name: GEP SMART Procurement Platform API
what_it_does: Enterprise procurement and supply chain platform with REST API — providing source-to-pay (S2P) automation covering strategic sourcing, contract management, supplier management, purchase order management, accounts payable, and spend analytics for Fortune 500 procurement teams, with AI-powered spend classification and supplier risk scoring.
use_cases: Integrating GEP SMART procurement data with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle) via API for PO and invoice sync
Querying supplier information, contracts, and spend analytics via GEP SMART API for reporting
Automating purchase order creation and approval workflows via GEP SMART API
Syncing supplier onboarding and qualification data between GEP SMART and master data systems
Extracting procurement analytics and savings tracking data via API for executive dashboards
not_for: Small business procurement (GEP SMART targets Fortune 500 with complex procurement needs)
Simple purchase order management (use QuickBooks or Procurify for SMB needs)
Consumer purchasing (GEP SMART is enterprise B2B procurement only)
best_when: A Fortune 500 procurement organization needs a unified source-to-pay platform with AI-powered spend analytics, supplier risk management, and deep ERP integration — especially for complex global procurement with multi-currency, multi-language requirements.
avoid_when: You're not a large enterprise, have simple procurement needs, lack the procurement team bandwidth for implementation, or don't have budget for enterprise software.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for system integrations. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped with module-level access controls. Webhook for procurement event notifications (PO approvals, invoice processing, supplier onboarding). REST API for procurement module integration. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Scoped by procurement module (sourcing, contracts, PO, AP). Integration with SAP, Oracle, and Workday via certified connectors.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; enterprise pricing only
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing; contact sales; typically $500K-2M+/year for large implementations
pricing.notes: New Jersey company (Clark, NJ). Founded 2000. Privately held. GEP is a Global 2000 procurement outsourcing and software company. SMART is their unified procurement platform. Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for Procurement Applications. 550+ enterprise clients. Unique: also offers managed services (BPO) alongside software — can handle procurement operations. Strong in manufacturing, retail, healthcare, financial services verticals. AI-powered spend classification using ML. Cloud-native on Microsoft Azure.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per contract; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for procurement module integration. OAuth2 client credentials auth. Enterprise documentation with ERP integration guides. Webhook for procurement events. Module-level access controls require careful configuration. SAP/Oracle/Workday certified connectors simplify ERP integration. Long implementation timeline (6-18 months for enterprise) before API integration is meaningful.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Data reads fully idempotent. PO creation requires idempotency key or external ID for deduplication. Supplier data reads idempotent. Contract reads idempotent by contract number.
known_agent_gotchas: Enterprise-only pricing and implementation — requires significant investment and time
Implementation timeline 6-18 months before API integration is production-ready
Module-level access controls require configuration per procurement module
Managed services offering complicates API boundaries — BPO team may handle some processes
Microsoft Azure hosting — verify data residency requirements per module

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Multi-region data residency. OAuth2 with module-level scopes. Enterprise-grade security standards. Procurement data is highly sensitive (supplier pricing, contracts, spend) — strict access controls required. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Microsoft Azure hosting with enterprise security controls. Supplier data subject to data processing agreements.
